attacking_style: Pure power sprinter launching from 200-500m out, often freelancing wheel-to-wheel rather than following structured leadout. Wins through raw acceleration and late bike throw timing rather than positional perfection.
team_tactics: Quick-Step (2017) provided SABATINI-led leadout but Kittel frequently abandoned structure at 500m to freelance through gaps. Team often mistimed launches (500m too early). Post-Quick-Step (2018), lost positioning infrastructure and struggled without premium leadout.
terrain_preference: Pure flat bunch sprints only. Completely exposed on any rolling terrain or profile filtering — dropped with teammates on even mild undulations approaching finales. Cannot defend sprint position in mountains for green jersey points.
vulnerability: Rolling finales drop him even when not classified climbs. Messy/disrupted sprints after leaving Quick-Step infrastructure. Early season condition poor. Lost confidence navigating chaotic sprints without perfect leadout by 2018.
key_patterns: Won 5 Tour stages in 2017 through wheel-switching freelance style, abandoning leadout structure intentionally (4 observations)
Dropped on any rolling terrain approaching finish, green jersey vulnerability in non-flat stages (3 observations)
Sabatini leadout often launched 500m too early, forcing prolonged wind exposure before line (2 observations)
Dramatic decline after leaving Quick-Step infrastructure in 2018, positioning errors and confidence loss noted (3 observations)
Exceptional late-sprint acceleration and bike throw from behind, winning photo finishes from deficits (3 observations)
Early season form consistently poor, non-factor until mid-season peak condition (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2018 showed clear decline after team switch, lost positioning ability and confidence. Went from 5 Tour stage wins (2017) to not reaching top 30 positioning and struggling in disrupted sprints.
